prompt:
Create a clean black-line-art medical-illustration in the universal patient-information-leaflet style: uniform medium-weight black ink lines on pure white background, no color, no frame, no border, no captions, no labels other than the embossed text on the tube itself, no logos, no numbers.

Subject: a single standalone illustration of an ASSEMBLED PROTECTOR TUBE shown STANDING UPRIGHT (cap on top, body on bottom) in a 1:1 square composition, centered, with generous clean white margins. NO hands, NO vial, NO arrow, NO bag, NO other objects.

=== TUBE SHAPE ===
A short stout cylindrical container, drawn in 3/4 perspective view (showing the cylindrical front face plus a hint of the top cap surface and one curved side). Total assembled height ≈ 1.3x the tube's diameter — STUBBY AND CHUNKY, like a fat short pill bottle, NOT a tall slim test tube. Two sections stacked:

  • BLACK CAP — TOP section. A short squat solid-black cylinder. Cap height (from its top edge down to the cap/body seam) is about 0.5x the cap's diameter. Solid black fill. The cap is positioned on top of the body, with a clean horizontal seam where they meet.

  • WHITE BODY — BOTTOM section. A stubby white cylinder of the SAME DIAMETER as the cap. Body height (from cap/body seam down to its base) is about 0.9x its diameter. Line-art outline only, no fill (white interior).

The cap and body share the SAME WIDE DIAMETER and together form one continuous chunky cylinder.

=== EMBOSSED TEXT — LETTER-BY-LETTER POSITIONS ===

Imagine each section divided into 6 EQUAL HORIZONTAL ZONES from top to bottom (zone 1 = topmost, zone 6 = bottommost).

**BLACK CAP — embossed 'DIALED':**
  • Zone 1 (TOPMOST zone of the cap, near the very top edge of the cap): the letter D
  • Zone 2 (just below zone 1): the letter I
  • Zone 3 (middle-upper of the cap): the letter A
  • Zone 4 (middle-lower of the cap): the letter L
  • Zone 5 (just above the cap/body seam): the letter E
  • Zone 6 (BOTTOMMOST zone of the cap, immediately above the cap/body seam): the letter D

Reading the cap from TOP TO BOTTOM (zone 1 → zone 6), the letters in order are: **D, I, A, L, E, D** = the word 'DIALED' spelled forward.

The first D (the topmost letter of the word 'DIALED') sits at the TOP of the cap. The last D (the final letter of the word 'DIALED') sits at the BOTTOM of the cap.

Each letter is rotated 90° from upright (sideways, like text on a book spine — letter tops face one side of the cylinder, letter bottoms face the other). To read the letters as right-side-up text, a viewer tilts their head 90°. Letters are rendered as thin WHITE outline strokes against the solid-black cap fill (line-art embossed look, letter interior remains black, NOT solid white blocks). Bold sans-serif capital letterforms.

**WHITE BODY — embossed 'HEALTH':**
  • Zone 1 (TOPMOST zone of the body, just below the cap/body seam): the letter H
  • Zone 2: the letter E
  • Zone 3: the letter A
  • Zone 4: the letter L
  • Zone 5: the letter T
  • Zone 6 (BOTTOMMOST zone of the body, near its base): the letter H

Reading the body from TOP TO BOTTOM (zone 1 → zone 6), the letters in order are: **H, E, A, L, T, H** = the word 'HEALTH' spelled forward.

The first H (the topmost letter of the word 'HEALTH') sits at the TOP of the body. The last H (the final letter of the word 'HEALTH') sits at the BOTTOM of the body.

Each letter rotated 90° from upright (book-spine style), thin BLACK outline strokes against the white body (line-art embossed look, letter interior remains white, NOT solid black blocks). Bold sans-serif capital letterforms.

=== COMBINED READING ===
Reading the entire upright assembled tube TOP-TO-BOTTOM, letter by letter, you encounter twelve letters in this exact sequence: D, I, A, L, E, D, H, E, A, L, T, H = 'DIALED HEALTH' spelled forward.

=== REFERENCE ===
The provided reference image (protector tube top.png) shows the real assembled protector tube laid on its SIDE for context. The text orientation and tube shape must match this product when the tube is mentally rotated 90° clockwise to stand upright (cap on top). The first D of DIALED was at the LEFT (cap-end) of the horizontal product photo, so after the 90° CW rotation it sits at the TOP of the upright cap. The first H of HEALTH was at the cap-end of the body in the photo, so after rotation it sits at the TOP of the upright body.

=== STYLE ===
Uniform medium-weight black ink line work on pure white background. Minimal short parallel hatching only for soft shadow / volume on the curved surfaces. 1:1 square composition. Centered subject, generous clean white margins. Drawn-by-hand line work.

No frame, no border, no caption, no labels other than the embossed DIALED on the cap and HEALTH on the body, no logos, no numbers, no hands, no other objects.
refImages: DIaled Health Instruction Graphics/Product Refs/protector tube top.png
